description: Client Management Backend — .NET 10 Web API for managing salon clients with full CRUD and soft-delete to preserve booking history

userStories.title: Client entity, EF Core configuration, and migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Client domain entity, EF Core configuration, and a database migration so that client data can be persisted.
acceptanceCriteria: src/backend/Chairly.Domain/Entities/Client.cs created with properties: Id (Guid), TenantId (Guid), FirstName (string), LastName (string), Email (string?), PhoneNumber (string?), Notes (string?), CreatedAtUtc (DateTimeOffset), CreatedBy (Guid), UpdatedAtUtc (DateTimeOffset?), UpdatedBy (Guid?), DeletedAtUtc (DateTimeOffset?), DeletedBy (Guid?)
No EF Core dependency in Chairly.Domain

src/backend/Chairly.Infrastructure/Persistence/Configurations/ClientConfiguration.cs created implementing IEntityTypeConfiguration<Client>: table name 'Clients', TenantId required, FirstName required MaxLength(100), LastName required MaxLength(100), Email optional MaxLength(256), PhoneNumber optional MaxLength(50), Notes optional MaxLength(1000), CreatedBy required, UpdatedBy optional, DeletedBy optional, index on TenantId
ChairlyDbContext.cs updated: add 'public DbSet<Client> Clients => Set<Client>();'

EF Core migration 'AddClient' created by running: dotnet ef migrations add AddClient -p src/backend/Chairly.Infrastructure/Chairly.Infrastructure.csproj -s src/backend/Chairly.Api/Chairly.Api.csproj (run from repo root)
.gitkeep removed from Chairly.Api/Features/Clients/ when real files are added in subsequent stories
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: Follow the same pattern as StaffMember entity in Chairly.Domain/Entities/StaffMember.cs and its configuration in Chairly.Infrastructure/Persistence/Configurations/StaffMemberConfiguration.cs. No enum fields on Client — no HasConversion needed. Run dotnet format before --verify-no-changes to auto-fix any encoding issues. If migration creation fails due to missing connection string, add a design-time factory: src/backend/Chairly.Infrastructure/ChairlyDbContextFactory.cs implementing IDesignTimeDbContextFactory<ChairlyDbContext> — check if this file already exists first.

userStories.title: ClientResponse DTO and GetClientsList endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a GET /api/clients endpoint that returns all non-deleted clients for the current tenant so that the frontend can display the client list.
acceptanceCriteria: src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Clients/ClientResponse.cs created: internal sealed record ClientResponse(Guid Id, string FirstName, string LastName, string? Email, string? PhoneNumber, string? Notes, DateTimeOffset CreatedAtUtc, DateTimeOffset? UpdatedAtUtc)
src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Clients/GetClientsList/GetClientsListQuery.cs created: internal sealed record GetClientsListQuery() : IRequest<IEnumerable<ClientResponse>>

src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Clients/GetClientsList/GetClientsListHandler.cs created: queries db.Clients where TenantId == TenantConstants.DefaultTenantId AND DeletedAtUtc == null, orders by LastName then FirstName, maps to ClientResponse[]
src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Clients/GetClientsList/GetClientsListEndpoint.cs created: MapGet on '/' returning Results.Ok(result)
src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Clients/ClientEndpoints.cs created: static class with MapClientEndpoints extension method that creates a group on '/api/clients' and calls MapGetClientsList()
Program.cs updated: add 'app.MapClientEndpoints();' after app.MapStaffEndpoints()

src/backend/Chairly.Tests/Features/Clients/ClientHandlerTests.cs created with at least 1 test: GetClientsListHandler_ReturnsOnlyNonDeletedClientsForTenant — seeds one active and one soft-deleted client, asserts only the active one is returned
#pragma warning disable CA1812 on all handler classes
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: Follow the GetStaffListHandler pattern. TenantId filtering: use TenantConstants.DefaultTenantId from Chairly.Api.Shared.Tenancy (check how GetStaffListHandler does it). Add #pragma warning disable CA1812 on all handler classes (instantiated via DI reflection). Delete the Chairly.Api/Features/Clients/.gitkeep file when creating real files. Use StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase on any new Dictionary constructors (MA0002).

userStories.title: CreateClient endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a POST /api/clients endpoint that creates a new client so that salon staff can register new clients.
acceptanceCriteria: src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Clients/CreateClient/CreateClientCommand.cs created implementing IRequest<ClientResponse> with: FirstName (string, [Required][MaxLength(100)]), LastName (string, [Required][MaxLength(100)]), Email (string?, [EmailAddress] validation attribute), PhoneNumber (string?, [MaxLength(50)]), Notes (string?, [MaxLength(1000)])

src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Clients/CreateClient/CreateClientHandler.cs created: validates command using Validator.TryValidateObject, creates Client entity with TenantId=TenantConstants.DefaultTenantId, Id=Guid.NewGuid(), CreatedAtUtc=DateTimeOffset.UtcNow, CreatedBy=Guid.Empty, saves to DB, returns ClientResponse via internal static ToResponse(Client) method
CreateClientHandler.ToResponse(Client client) is internal static so it can be reused by UpdateClientHandler and DeleteClientHandler within the same bounded context
src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Clients/CreateClient/CreateClientEndpoint.cs created: POST /api/clients, returns Results.Created('/api/clients/{id}', result)
ClientEndpoints.cs updated to register the CreateClient endpoint

ClientHandlerTests.cs updated with at least 2 tests: CreateClientHandler_HappyPath_CreatesAndReturnsClient, CreateClientHandler_MissingFirstName_ReturnsValidationError (or assert validation attribute directly)
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: Follow CreateStaffMemberHandler pattern. No enum parsing needed — Client has no enums. The ToResponse() static method maps Client entity fields directly. Use Guid.Empty for CreatedBy (with #pragma warning disable MA0026 if the analyzer fires). Validation: use System.ComponentModel.DataAnnotations.Validator.TryValidateObject to manually validate the command before processing — this is consistent with existing handlers.

userStories.title: UpdateClient endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a PUT /api/clients/{id} endpoint that updates an existing client's information.
acceptanceCriteria: src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Clients/UpdateClient/UpdateClientCommand.cs created implementing IRequest<OneOf<ClientResponse, NotFound>> with: Id (Guid), FirstName (string, [Required][MaxLength(100)]), LastName (string, [Required][MaxLength(100)]), Email (string?, [EmailAddress]), PhoneNumber (string?, [MaxLength(50)]), Notes (string?, [MaxLength(1000)])

src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Clients/UpdateClient/UpdateClientHandler.cs created: fetches client by Id and TenantId where DeletedAtUtc == null, returns NotFound if not found or already deleted, validates command, updates FirstName/LastName/Email/PhoneNumber/Notes, sets UpdatedAtUtc=DateTimeOffset.UtcNow and UpdatedBy=Guid.Empty, saves, returns ClientResponse via CreateClientHandler.ToResponse()
src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Clients/UpdateClient/UpdateClientEndpoint.cs created: PUT /api/clients/{id:guid}, returns Results.Ok on success, Results.NotFound on NotFound
ClientEndpoints.cs updated to register the UpdateClient endpoint
ClientHandlerTests.cs updated with at least 2 tests: UpdateClientHandler_HappyPath_UpdatesAndReturnsClient, UpdateClientHandler_NotFound_ReturnsNotFound
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: Follow UpdateStaffMemberHandler pattern. Reuse CreateClientHandler.ToResponse() (internal static, same bounded context — import using Chairly.Api.Features.Clients.CreateClient). The endpoint receives both route id and body: bind Guid id from route and UpdateClientCommand from body, set command.Id = id before calling mediator.Send(command). A deleted client (DeletedAtUtc != null) cannot be updated — return NotFound in that case.

userStories.title: DeleteClient endpoint (soft delete)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a DELETE /api/clients/{id} endpoint that soft-deletes a client so that their booking history is preserved.
acceptanceCriteria: src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Clients/DeleteClient/DeleteClientCommand.cs created implementing IRequest<OneOf<Success, NotFound, Conflict>> with: Id (Guid)

src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Clients/DeleteClient/DeleteClientHandler.cs created: fetches client by Id and TenantId (including already-deleted ones — do NOT filter by DeletedAtUtc == null), returns NotFound if client does not exist, returns Conflict (idempotency) if DeletedAtUtc != null, sets DeletedAtUtc=DateTimeOffset.UtcNow and DeletedBy=Guid.Empty (with MA0026 pragma if needed), saves, returns Success

src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Clients/DeleteClient/DeleteClientEndpoint.cs created: DELETE /api/clients/{id:guid}, returns Results.NoContent() on Success, Results.NotFound() on NotFound, Results.Conflict() on Conflict
ClientEndpoints.cs updated to register the DeleteClient endpoint

ClientHandlerTests.cs updated with at least 3 tests: DeleteClientHandler_HappyPath_SetsDeletedAtUtc (asserts client.DeletedAtUtc is non-null after delete), DeleteClientHandler_NotFound_ReturnsNotFound, DeleteClientHandler_AlreadyDeleted_ReturnsConflict (idempotency: re-deleting returns Conflict without overwriting original DeletedAtUtc)
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: Follow DeactivateStaffMemberHandler pattern for idempotency guard. The key difference from deactivate: soft-delete is permanent (no reactivate endpoint). Use OneOf<Success, NotFound, Conflict> — check how other handlers return Success (no payload). The Conflict type may be from OneOf library or a custom result type — check what exists in the codebase. The fetch query must NOT filter out deleted clients (to detect the already-deleted case): db.Clients.FirstOrDefaultAsync(c => c.Id == command.Id && c.TenantId == tenantId) — no DeletedAtUtc filter here.

userStories.title: Add sorting test for GetClientsList
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a test that verifies GetClientsList returns clients sorted by LastName then FirstName so that the sort order guarantee is covered by tests.
acceptanceCriteria: ClientHandlerTests.cs updated with a new test: GetClientsListHandler_ReturnsSortedByLastNameThenFirstName
Test seeds at least 3 clients with different LastName/FirstName combinations inserted in a non-sorted order
Test asserts the returned list is ordered by LastName ascending, then FirstName ascending (e.g. 'Bakker, Anna' before 'Bakker, Piet' before 'de Vries, Jan')
All seeded clients are non-deleted and belong to TenantConstants.DefaultTenantId
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: Add the test to the existing ClientHandlerTests.cs alongside the other handler tests. Insert clients in a deliberately non-alphabetical order (e.g. 'de Vries' first, then 'Bakker, Piet', then 'Bakker, Anna') so the test validates the ORDER BY rather than insertion order. Use the existing CreateTestClient helper. After this story, ALL stories are complete — follow the COMPLETE protocol in CLAUDE.md: push to the existing branch, then wait for CI.
